answers: Debate is a formal discussion where two sides argue for or against a topic using logic, evidence, and persuasion. It builds critical thinking and communication skills.
Debate jitote chaile: tomar topic research koro, strong evidence use koro, opponent er point anticipate koro, ar confident theko. Emotional na hoye logical theko.
Types of debate include parliamentary, Lincoln-Douglas, policy debate, and informal debates. Each has different formats and rules, but all require research, logic, and clear communication.
To improve debate skills, practice arguing both sides of an issue, read widely, learn logical fallacies so you can spot them, and listen carefully to your opponent before responding.
